basic_details.job_title: Indian Air Force Pharmacist Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online For Airmen Group Y Medical Assistant Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Indian Air Force (Central Airmen Selection Board - CASB)
basic_details.short_information: The Indian Air Force (IAF) has released the Airmen Recruitment Notification 2026 inviting online applications from male Indian/Gorkha (a subject of Nepal) candidates for the Group 'Y' (Non-Technical) Medical Assistant trade under Airmen Intake 02/2027. This recruitment covers both 10+2 qualified candidates and candidates holding a Diploma/B.Pharma/B.Sc in Pharmacy, commonly searched as IAF Pharmacist Recruitment. Online registration runs from 06 July 2026 to 26 July 2026, and the online exam is scheduled on 22-23 September 2026.

application_fee.details: This section answers a commonly searched query about how much candidates need to pay to submit their online application for this recruitment. | Examination Fee: Rs. 550/- GST Applicable: 18% on the examination fee Total Fee Nature: Non-refundable under any circumstance Payment Mode: Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, UPI

age_limit.details: Candidate Category Maximum Age on Date of Enrolment 10+2 / Intermediate / Equivalent (incl. vocational course) 21 Years Diploma / B.Pharma / B.Sc in Pharmacy 24 Years

qualification.details: This section details the minimum academic requirements that determine whether a candidate qualifies for the Medical Assistant trade under either eligibility stream. | Candidates should have passed 10+2/Intermediate/Equivalent with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English from a Board recognised by Central, State, or UT authorities, securing a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate and 50% marks in English. Alternatively, a two-year vocational course with non-vocational subjects of Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English with the same percentage criteria is also accepted. | Candidates must have passed Intermediate/10+2/Equivalent with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate and 50% marks in English, in addition to holding a Diploma/B.Pharma/B.Sc in Pharmacy with at least 50% aggregate marks and valid registration with the State Pharmacy Council or Pharmacy Council of India (PCI) at the time of enrolment. | Note: The exact aggregate percentage before decimal, as printed on the marksheet, will be considered — for example, 49.99% will be treated as 49% and not rounded off to 50%.

selection_process: Test Maximum Time (up to 21 years) Maximum Time (above 21 / Diploma-Pharma candidates)
1.6 Km Run Within 7 minutes Within 7 minutes 30 seconds

The selection procedure for this Indian Air Force Pharmacist Recruitment 2026 is conducted in three phases. This section explains what each phase involves and how candidates progress through the process.
Duration: 45 minutes Subjects: English (as per 10+2 CBSE syllabus) and Reasoning & General Awareness (RAGA) Marking: +1 for every correct answer
-0.25 for each wrong answer Candidates must qualify in each paper separately; marks may be normalised across sessions
Candidates who clear the run undergo PFT-II after a 10-minute recuperation break

Candidates who pass PFT undertake Adaptability Test-I (objective written test) Candidates clearing Adaptability Test-I proceed to Adaptability Test-II as per current policy Document verification of educational and other certificates is also carried out at this stage
Candidates who qualify Adaptability Test-II are issued a medical appointment letter and undergo a detailed medical examination conducted by the Air Force Medical Team
Candidates declared medically unfit may apply for an Appeal Medical Board (AMB) by depositing Rs. 40/- within three working days of the medical examination.

physical_eligibility.visual acuity: 6/36 each eye, correctable to 6/6 each eye
physical_eligibility.minimum chest circumference: 77 cm with at least 5 cm expansion
physical_eligibility.hearing: Able to hear forced whisper from 6 metres, each ear separately
physical_eligibility.dental: Minimum 14 dental points; orthodontic appliances not permitted
physical_eligibility.details: Apart from academic eligibility, candidates must meet specific medical and physical fitness standards laid down by the IAF. This section summarises the key medical parameters applicants should be aware of before applying. | Additional conditions include a zero-tolerance policy on narcotic drugs and alcoholism, restrictions on permanent body tattoos (except specific arm/hand locations as per policy), and disqualification for candidates with a history of LASIK/PRK surgery.

how_to_apply: This section provides the step-by-step process to successfully register and submit the online application on the official CASB web portal.
Visit the official CASB recruitment portal at https://iafrecruitment.edcil.co.in
Complete Step-I registration by entering personal and contact details, then generate and verify OTPs sent to your e-mail ID and mobile number
Receive your User ID and Password via e-mail/SMS, then click "Go To Application"
Complete Step-II by filling Personal Details, Communication Details, Qualification Details, Declaration, and uploading required documents
Upload scanned photograph (100-200 KB), signature (80-150 KB), left thumb impression (50-100 KB), and parent's signature if below 18 years of age, all in .jpg/.jpeg format
Capture a live image for facial verification against the uploaded photograph
Preview the filled application and correct any errors before final submission
Pay the non-refundable examination fee of Rs. 550/- plus 18% GST through the Payment Gateway
Print and save the submitted application form in PDF for future reference

Candidates can use the Apply Online facility only between 06 July 2026, 1100 Hr and 26 July 2026, 2300 Hr. Each candidate should apply only once; multiple applications will lead to rejection of candidature.
important_instructions: This section highlights additional rules candidates must follow while applying and appearing for the selection test.

Candidates must indicate five choices of examination city and fourteen choices of ASC for Phase-II in order of preference Requests for change of examination centre or date will not be entertained Mobile phones, smart watches, and electronic devices are strictly prohibited in testing areas during Phase-I and Phase-II Candidates discharged from the Indian Air Force for any reason are not eligible to reapply No bribe is required at any stage; candidates should beware of unscrupulous agents posing as recruiters Any corrigendum or update will be published only on the CASB web portal, not in newspapers or other media

faq.question: Q1. Has the Indian Air Force (IAF) released the Airmen Recruitment Notification 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, the IAF has released the recruitment notification for Airmen Intake 02/2027 under the Group 'Y' Medical Assistant trade, inviting online applications from 06 July 2026 to 26 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. Who can apply for the IAF Medical Assistant/Pharmacist trade?
faq.answer: Male Indian citizens and Gorkha subjects of Nepal who meet the educational qualification and age criteria for either the 10+2 stream or the Diploma/B.Pharma/B.Sc in Pharmacy stream can apply.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for IAF Pharmacist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For 10+2 candidates, the date of birth must fall between 01 July 2006 and 01 July 2010. For Diploma/B.Pharma/B.Sc Pharmacy candidates, unmarried applicants must be born between 01 July 2003 and 01 July 2008, while married applicants must be born between 01 July 2003 and 01 July 2006.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 550/- plus 18% GST, and it is non-refundable under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q5. When is the IAF online exam for Airmen Intake 02/2027?
faq.answer: The online examination (Phase-I) is scheduled on 22-23 September 2026.

faq.question: Q6. What is the syllabus for the IAF Airmen online test?
faq.answer: The Phase-I online test covers English (as per 10+2 CBSE syllabus) and Reasoning & General Awareness (RAGA), with a duration of 45 minutes.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary for an IAF Medical Assistant Airman?
faq.answer: During training, candidates receive a stipend of Rs. 14,600/- per month. After successful completion of training, the starting gross emoluments are Rs. 26,900/- per month, including Military Service Pay (MSP), plus other applicable allowances.

faq.question: Q8. What is the minimum height required for this trade?
faq.answer: The minimum height required for Medical Assistant and Medical Assistant (Pharmacist) trades is 152 cms.

faq.question: Q9. What physical fitness test do candidates need to clear?
faq.answer: Candidates must complete a 1.6 Km run within 7 minutes (or 7 minutes 30 seconds for candidates above 21 years or holding Diploma/B.Pharma/B.Sc Pharmacy), followed by 10 push-ups, 10 sit-ups, and 20 squats, each within one minute.

faq.question: Q10. When will the Provisional Select List (PSL) be published?
faq.answer: The Provisional Select List will be displayed at all Airmen Selection Centres and on the CASB web portal in the last week of May 2027.

title: Indian Air Force Pharmacist Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online For Airmen Group Y Medical Assistant Posts
